title: Wall divided by a curvy line (monochrome, directional grain)

rule.instruction: A wall divided from the upper left to the lower right by a curvy line; left: glossy yellow; right: glossy purple.

restrictions: USER-PICKED. Original is a COLOUR piece (glossy yellow / glossy purple) — flagged with the user, who agreed to a MONOCHROME adaptation: keep the structure (a curvy line dividing the wall upper-left to lower-right into two regions), drop the colour, and distinguish the two sides by DIRECTIONAL GRAIN (contrasting line angles) instead. New `curvyDivide` generator: a bold hand-drawn curvy divide + each side filled with open, hand-drawn grain at a contrasting angle; the curvy seam is the hero. Kept LIGHT/OPEN per the session's density lesson (generous spacing, hand wobble, negative feel) — not a dense weave, not mechanical. Monochrome black on white. The 16 span curve wildness (curviness/waves), grain-angle contrast (perpendicular / horizontal-vs-vertical / subtle), and openness (spacing).
